result:
Done. The API is built, tested end-to-end, and committed.

## What was built

A JSON REST API (Node ESM, no web framework â€” just the built-in `http` server) backed by an on-disk **SQLite** file via `node:sqlite`:

- **`db.js`** â€” opens/creates the SQLite file (`data.sqlite`, override with `DB_PATH`) and the `users`, `sessions`, `notes` tables.
- **`store.js`** â€” data access + auth. Passwords are **scrypt-hashed with per-user salt** (never stored or returned in plaintext); sessions are 256-bit opaque bearer tokens persisted in the DB.
- **`server.js`** â€” routing and request handling.

### Endpoints
| Route | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `POST /signup` | validates non-empty `username`/`password` â†’ `201 {id, username}` (no password); duplicate â†’ `409`; bad payload â†’ `400` |
| `POST /login` | correct creds â†’ `200 {session}`; wrong/unknown â†’ `401`; bad payload â†’ `400` |
| `GET /me` | bearer token â†’ `200 {id, username}`; missing/invalid â†’ `401` |
| `POST /notes` | bearer + `{title, body}` â†’ `201 {id, title, body}`; unauth â†’ `401` |
| `GET /notes` | bearer â†’ `200` array of **only that user's** notes; unauth â†’ `401` |

Listens on `PORT` (default 3000). Added the `start` script (`node server.js`).

### Verification
The end-to-end run confirmed every status code (201/400/409/401/200), that passwords are excluded from responses, that alice and bob each see only their own notes, and that **after killing and restarting the server, alice's user and note were still present** â€” persistence across restart works. The `.gitignore` was updated so the SQLite data files aren't committed.
